data.issue: Root causes for high defect count

factors.description: Calibration drift explains 35.7% of defects. Progressive drift leads to placement errors and dimensional defects.
Machine vibration contributes 27.1% to defect occurrence. High vibration causes mechanical misalignment during soldering.
Temperature accounts for 9.5% of quality variation. Excursions beyond 85°C degrade solder joint quality.
Production speed accounts for 6.9% of quality issues. Excessive speed reduces inspection accuracy.
Shift timing accounts for 6.9%. Night shifts show elevated defect rates.
Humidity contributes 5.0% to defect rate. High moisture causes oxidation and poor solder wetting.
Power consumption pattern explains 4.5%. Anomalous power draw indicates equipment issues.
Pressure variation contributes 4.4%. Unstable pressure affects component placement force.

data.methodology: Gradient Boosting Feature Importance + SHAP Decomposition
